creator: bhargava.marreddy@broadcom.com
text:
I'm not seeing any issue on my DELL R730.
Please double check the configuration on Link partner (i.e. physical switch)?
If possible, please try by connecting back-to-back (to avoid switch issues).

If the problem still exists, please provide o/p of following commands.
## cat /var/log/messages > logs.txt
## sysctl -a > sysctl.txt
## ifconfig -m > ifconfig.txt

Snip from my setup ==>

#dmidecode-1.8 | grep ..
Handle 0x0100
        DMI type 1, 27 bytes.
        System Information Block
                Vendor: Dell Inc.
                Product: PowerEdge R730
                Version:
                Serial Number: GH1YQG2

# ifconfig -m bge3
bge3: flags=8843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> metric 0 mtu 1500
        options=c019b<RXCSUM,TXCSUM,VLAN_MTU,VLAN_HWTAGGING,VLAN_HWCSUM,TSO4,VLAN_HWTSO,LINKSTATE>
        capabilities=c019b<RXCSUM,TXCSUM,VLAN_MTU,VLAN_HWTAGGING,VLAN_HWCSUM,TSO4,VLAN_HWTSO,LINKSTATE>
        ether 18:66:da:ed:1a:8d
        hwaddr 18:66:da:ed:1a:8d
        inet 10.130.210.24 netmask 0xfffffe00 broadcast 10.130.211.255
        nd6 options=29<PERFORMNUD,IFDISABLED,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>
        media: Ethernet autoselect (1000baseT <full-duplex>)
        status: active
        supported media:
                media autoselect mediaopt flowcontrol
                media autoselect
                media 1000baseT mediaopt full-duplex,master
                media 1000baseT mediaopt full-duplex
                media 1000baseT mediaopt master
                media 1000baseT
                media 100baseTX mediaopt full-duplex
                media 100baseTX
                media 10baseT/UTP mediaopt full-duplex
                media 10baseT/UTP
